item.title: Northern California hit by 5.6 magnitude earthquake in Mendocino County
item.description: A 5.6 magnitude earthquake struck Mendocino County on Wednesday morning, triggering ShakeAlert notifications across Northern California.

item.title: At least 32 dead, hundreds hurt in 2 strong Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: Acting President Delcy Rodríguez​ says the numbers are expected to rise. The temblors hit within a minute of each other. Devastating damage is reported.

item.title: U.N. nuclear agency chief says Iran inspections will happen, at some point
item.description: After conflicting remarks from Iran and the U.S., the U.N. nuclear agency chief says Iranian sites will be inspected, but the timing is "not essential."

item.title: Trump has testy meeting with GOP senators, telling Cassidy to sit down
item.description: President Trump met with Republican senators soon after canceling plans to sign bipartisan housing affordability legislation at the Capitol.

item.title: Massive fire in Allentown prompts evacuations for nearby residents
item.description: A massive fire in Allentown, Pennsylvania, has forced nearby residents to evacuate their homes Wednesday night.

item.title: Senate rejects measure to restrict Trump's Iran war powers as key Republicans shift
item.description: The Senate late Wednesday rejected a measure aimed at restricting President Trump's power to wage war against Iran, a victory for Senate GOP leadership — and a shift from one day earlier.

item.title: Trump admin. asks Congress for $88B in supplemental funds for Iran war, other needs
item.description: The Trump administration on Wednesday sent Congress a long-awaited supplemental funding package to help cover the cost of the Iran war.

item.title: See the aftermath of the Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: Two earthquakes hit Venezuela on Wednesday, the first measuring a magnitude of 7.2 and the second 7.5. The country's acting President, Delcy Rodriguez, declared a state of emergency. Videos show the heavy damage in the capital of Caracas as rescue crews look for survivors.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Air Canada flight diverted to Logan Airport due to "incapacitated" captain
item.description: An Air Canada flight out of Newark, New Jersey, was forced to divert and land at Boston after the captain became "incapacitated" and a co-pilot had to take over.

item.title: California teacher pregnancy leave bill could create paid leave for abortions
item.description: Gov. Newsom's proposed funding for teacher pregnancy leave currently includes paid leave for abortion procedures.

item.title: Military requiring flu vaccines for recruits as Air Force base deals with outbreak
item.description: All military branches began requiring recruits to get flu vaccines earlier this month, an exception to Pete Hegseth's decision to lift the military's vaccine mandate, a Pentagon official said.

item.title: Back-to-back powerful earthquakes hit Venezuela
item.description: Two strong earthquakes hit west of Venezuela's capital on Wednesday. Scientists are estimating high casualties and extensive damage. BBC News' Vanessa Silva has the latest from Caracas.

item.title: Previewing the U.S. match against Turkey in the World Cup
item.description: The USMNT will take on Turkey Thursday in its final group stage match before heading to the knockout round. CBS News correspondent Nicole Valdes reports from the team's base camp in California.

item.title: U.S. aims to break AI reliance on China with Pax Silica Accord
item.description: The U.S. is trying to break the West's reliance on Chinese artificial intelligence supply chains with an international accord called Pax Silica. Its goal is to shore up supplies of essential components used in high-end computer chips, which power advanced AI models. CBS News coordinating producer Richard Escobedo has more.

item.title: Here's what Miranda Rights are and why they are important
item.description: CBS News legal contributor Jessica Levinson takes a closer look at Miranda Rights and how they factored into the Supreme Court's decision to reinstate the murder conviction against Pedro Hernandez in the 1979 disappearance of 6-year-old Etan Patz.

item.title: What happened at Trump's tense meeting with Republican senators
item.description: President Trump held a tense meeting with Senate Republicans on Wednesday, hours after he suddenly canceled a signing ceremony for newly passed housing affordability legislation. CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has the details. Then, New York Times congressional correspondent Robert Jimison joins to discuss further.

item.title: Where NATO alliance stands as Trump meets with Secretary-General Mark Rutte
item.description: President Trump met with NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte on Wednesday. Spencer Faragasso, senior fellow with the Institute for Science and International Security, joins with analysis.

item.title: Gen. Chris Donahue set to retire, in latest departure by top military official
item.description: Gen. Chris Donahue had earned the ire of Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, multiple sources told CBS News.

item.title: Gen. Christopher Donahue retires, forced out by Hegseth
item.description: One of the most decorated, combat-tested and respected members of the U.S. Armed Forces, Gen. Christopher Donahue, is retiring, forced out by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth. He's now one of more than a dozen senior officers who have been recently shown the door. Margaret Brennan reports.

item.title: 7.1 magnitude earthquake in Venezuela triggers tsunami alerts
item.description: An earthquake preliminarily measured as 7.1 magnitude was reported in northwest Venezuela Wednesday evening, the USGS says. Meteorologist Zoe Mintz has more.

item.title: Trump-backed candidate declared winner of Colombia's presidential runoff
item.description: Abelardo de la Espriella, a millionaire political newcomer, has been declared Colombia's next president.

item.title: 6/24: CBS Evening News
item.description: President Trump's luncheon with Republican senators devolves into shouting match; earthquake shakes Northern California.

item.title: Cait Conley on her race against Rep. Mike Lawler in November
item.description: In November, Republican Rep. Mike Lawler will face combat veteran and Biden administration national security staffer Cait Conley, who prevailed in Tuesday night's Democratic primary for New York's 17th congressional district. Conley joins "The Takeout" to discuss why she thinks she can flip the seat from red to blue.

item.title: CBS News poll finds more oppose than favor data centers in their area
item.description: Most Americans expect data centers to negatively impact the environment, local resources, although some see economic benefits

item.title: Bill Gates said Epstein tried to use knowledge of his affairs "to pressure me"
item.description: Bill Gates testified June 10 for nearly six hours before the House Oversight Committee, which is examining the government's handling of the Epstein case and those with ties to him.

item.title: Watch: Trump meets with NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte at Oval Office
item.description: President Trump hosted NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte at the White House on Wednesday. The pair discussed defense spending and more.

item.title: Trump calls for probe into gas price gouging
item.description: President Trump is calling on the Department of Justice to investigate what he calls gas price gouging at the pump. Patrick De Haan, head of petroleum analysis at GasBuddy, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: 6/24: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Trump and Cassidy have tense exchange in meeting with GOP senators; Mamdani-backed candidates win in New York primaries.

item.title: Trump to ask Congress to make 15% ethanol-gas blend permanent
item.description: E15 is usually only available part of the year to help ease high gas prices under a waiver from the EPA.
